evals.prompt: Wir sind eine 12-Mann IT-Beratung in München. Unser Geschäftsführer will auf LinkedIn mehr Sichtbarkeit, postet aber nur alle 2 Wochen was und wir haben eine eigene Firmen-Seite die wir auch bespielen wollen. Was empfehlt ihr?
evals.expected_output: Should clearly recommend Personal Brand of Geschäftsführer as primary channel — explain why Unternehmensseite doesn't convert in DACH B2B. Should propose posting cadence of 3-4x/week with 5-10 substanzielle Kommentare/Tag. Should ask whether the audience expects Sie or Du tone (IT-Beratung München = likely Sie). Should reference IMPORT/05 section 5.2.1 and the 360-Brew model adaptation. Should warn against posting 1-2x/month (algorithmus-kill).

evals.prompt: Wir posten seit 6 Monaten auf unserer Firmen-LinkedIn-Seite. Impressions: 2.000/Monat. Engagement: minimal. Sollen wir aufgeben?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT recommend giving up but should diagnose root cause: (1) likely Personal Brand vs. Firmenseite mismatch, (2) algorithm favors Personal Profiles, (3) 2k impressions is normal for inactive company pages. Should propose migration to Personal Brand of a named decision-maker, with company page as secondary amplifier. Should recommend repurposing existing content for Personal Brand. Should set realistic expectations: 3-6 months to meaningful reach.
assertions: Diagnoses Personal Brand vs Firmenseite issue

evals.prompt: Schreibe mir 5 LinkedIn-Post-Hooks für unsere KI-Automatisierungs-Agentur. Zielgruppe: Geschäftsführer 5-50 MA KMU.
evals.expected_output: Should produce 5 DACH-specific hooks following the Hook → Rehook → Payoff → CTA pattern from brand-voice.md. Should use the documented example patterns (Zahl/Schmerz, konträres Statement, Konkreter Schmerz). Should write in Sie-form for B2B. Should avoid buzzwords from brand-voice.md verbotene Liste. Should save them or present them numbered 1-5. Each hook should be 150-250 words total if expanded.
assertions: Follows Hook → Rehook → Payoff → CTA pattern

evals.prompt: Was ist der Unterschied zwischen LinkedIn Ads und organischen Posts bei uns? Wir haben 8.000€ monatlich für Marketing — wie allokieren wir das auf LinkedIn?
evals.expected_output: Should explain organic vs paid distinction: organic builds Personal Brand long-term, paid amplifies existing messages. Should NOT recommend specific budget splits (50€/Tag minimum, 80/20) — those are TODOs in the SKILL.md and not in the spec. Should ask Operator-Approval before giving budget recommendations. Should clarify that paid reach without good content is wasted money. Should mention DSGVO-Anforderungen für LinkedIn Lead-Gen Forms (handoff to dsgvo-lead-capture).
assertions: Explains organic vs paid distinction clearly
Does NOT recommend specific budget split (TODO)
Warns against paid without good content

evals.prompt: Unser Zielkunde ist 55+, traditionsbewusst, Steuerberater-Kanzlei. Die sagen 'LinkedIn ist nur was für junge Leute.' Wie überzeugen wir die?
evals.expected_output: Should acknowledge the misconception with empathy. Should explain that XING was German, but LinkedIn has won B2B in DACH since 2020. Should cite specific stat: 78% Social Sellers outperform peers. Should propose low-effort start: 2x/week posts + daily Kommentare (30 Min). Should suggest one specific use case relevant to Steuerberater (e.g. Mitarbeitergewinnung, Mandanten-Akquise). Should NOT push XING — clarify it lost B2B relevance.
assertions: Does NOT recommend XING as primary

evals.prompt: Wir posten schon, aber die Kommentare sind mies. 'Guter Beitrag!' oder 'Daumen hoch!' von unseren Mitarbeitern. Was machen wir falsch?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose 'comment inflation' — empty Kommentare signal the algorithmus doesn't see engagement depth. Should propose substanzielle Kommentar-Strategie: (1) add new info or counter-perspective, (2) ask follow-up question, (3) share personal experience, (4) minimum 3-4 sentences. Should reference the 360-Brew 'Pour' phase. Should warn against coordinating mass-likes among team (looks fake). Should suggest team training on kommentar-Etikette.
